era: 1986-1993. Punk Rock. E=8 -- raw, powerful, emotionally direct, musically grounded. Central District, Seattle.

location.origin: Yellow Springs, Ohio; relocated to Central District, Seattle, Washington

associated_musicians: Joan Jett (collaborated with The Gits survivors on tribute recordings)

theory_nodes.concept: Mia Zapata: one of the most gifted vocalists of the Pacific Northwest punk scene. Vocal range from intimate conversational to devastating full-throated. Blues dimension: note-bending, sustained vowels, silence before attack. Punk plus blues = something specifically hers. Irreplaceable.
theory_nodes.description: Mia Zapata's voice documented at appropriate depth as the engine's primary focus for this entry.

theory_nodes.concept: 'Frenching the Bully' (1992, C/Z Records, produced by Steve Albini): thirteen tracks, raw and direct, Zapata's voice at center of every dynamic shift. Albini production: minimal processing, close microphones, natural sound of instruments. The primary artistic statement.
theory_nodes.description: The Gits' sole full-length studio album documented as primary artistic statement.

theory_nodes.concept: E=8 FIFTH CONSECUTIVE (276-280): Bikini Kill, Sleater-Kinney, Unwound, Seven Year Bitch, The Gits. Most sustained E=8 streak in engine's Pacific Northwest documentation. Five bands, five consecutive degrees, five distinct characters within E=8.

C/Z RECORDS SECOND CONSECUTIVE (279-280): Seven Year Bitch and The Gits. Seattle independent label, distinct from Kill Rock Stars and Sub Pop. Capitol Hill-adjacent Seattle independent scene documented at two consecutive degrees.

HOME ALIVE (1993): non-profit organization founded in immediate aftermath of Mia Zapata's murder. Self-defense and personal safety resources for artists and community members. Free and low-cost classes. Most significant community organization to emerge from a music community tragedy in Seattle's history.
theory_nodes.description: Home Alive as community response documented.

theory_nodes.concept: Murder: Mia Zapata murdered July 7 1993, age 27, Central District Seattle. Case unsolved ten years. DNA evidence led to identification of Jesus Mezquia; conviction March 2004, Seattle; 36 years. Documented factually and briefly. Focus: the music and artistry.
theory_nodes.description: Murder documented factually, briefly, respectfully. Focus returns to music.

theory_nodes.concept: 279-280 bridge: '¡Viva Zapata!' (Seven Year Bitch, 1994) named for Mia Zapata. The most explicit cross-degree bridge in the engine's riot grrrl documentation. Joan Jett's lineage (276 Bikini Kill 'Rebel Girl' production) to The Gits (280 tribute collaboration): feminist punk solidarity across the PNW.
theory_nodes.description: The explicit bridge from 279 to 280 and the feminist punk lineage connecting 276 and 280.

cross_references.relationship: DIRECT BRIDGE. '¡Viva Zapata!' (1994) named for Mia Zapata. C/Z Records second consecutive degree. Two consecutive degrees connected by name, community, grief, and tribute.

Joan Jett produced 'Rebel Girl' (276) and collaborated with The Gits survivors (280). Feminist punk lineage as solidarity: the same musician connecting the most important riot grrrl band and the punk band whose vocalist was murdered.

Collaborated with The Gits' surviving members on tribute recordings. Joan Jett at both 276 (Bikini Kill production) and 280 (The Gits tribute): the feminist punk lineage expressed as sustained solidarity.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7. FIRST PHYSETERIDAE. Combined E=15. 230+ dB directional biosonar. Loudest biological sound on Earth. The Gits' raw punk directness paired with the sperm whale's powerful directional signal in the deep ocean.

milestones: MIA ZAPATA DOCUMENTED -- VOICE AND ARTISTRY FIRST
